title: Collect partner T-Echo 60s RF exchange evidence
description: Why this exists: partner observed a T-Echo sending a LICHEN Native node_info frame, but the observation is not yet enough to confirm bidirectional RF or close smoke validation. What needs to be done: collect the exact firmware commit, board/overlay, radio parameters, monitor command, raw log, decoded node_info payload, peer identity, RSSI/SNR if available, and at least a 60s log showing whether traffic is one-way or bidirectional. Acceptance criteria: evidence is attached in Beads notes or linked to a durable artifact; one-way versus bidirectional result is explicitly stated; resulting blocker or smoke-test Beads are updated.
notes: 2026-06-30 follow-up from partner chat: user asked whether the T-Echo node_info frame was the first LICHEN packet over actual RF. Partner response: hold off until bidirectional RX/TX between two nodes is confirmed. Current evidence remains one observed LICHEN Native node_info frame from T-Echo; 60s bidirectional exchange log is still required before closing smoke validation.
